name: Montgat-Tordera Trail
name_original: De Coll de Can Gurgui al Ermita de Sant Mateu del Bosc
description: Hike the easy 3.3-mile Montgat-Tordera Trail, gaining 521 feet, with views from Cornice Viewpoint and a visit to Sant Mateu del Bosc.
region_intro: Hiking trails around Premià De Dalt are situated within the scenic hills of the Maresme coast, nestled in the Serralada Litoral Natural Park. This region is characterized by extensive forests, primarily of pines and oaks, and an elevated position that provides panoramic views. The terrain includes varied paths, from accessible routes to those requiring good fitness, with elevation gains across its hills. Best hiking trails around Premià De Dalt The most popular hiking route is Montgat-Tordera Trail, a 3.2 miles (5.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 33 minutes to complete. This easy route offers views from Cornice Viewpoint and passes by Sant Mateu del Bosc. Another top favourite among local hikers is From Vallromanes to Ermita de Sant Mateu del Bosc, a moderate 6.5 miles (10.5 km) path. This route winds through the natural park's forests towards a historic hermitage. Local hikers also love the Turó de Sant Mateu – Cornice Viewpoint loop from Premià de Dalt, a 2.5 miles (4.0 km) trail leading through the hills of Serralada Litoral Natural Park, often completed in about 1 hour 8 minutes. Hiking around Premià De Dalt is defined by its mountainous coastal setting, extensive forests, and panoramic Mediterranean views. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Premià De Dalt are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 150 reviews. More than 1,300 hikers have used komoot to explore Premià De Dalt's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Premià De Dalt?
A: There are over 25 hiking routes in Premià De Dalt, offering a variety of experiences. These trails are situated within the scenic hills of the Maresme coast, nestled in the Serralada Litoral Natural Park.

Q: Are there any easy hikes suitable for beginners or families in Premià De Dalt?
A: Yes, Premià De Dalt offers several easy routes perfect for beginners or families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1323764376/montgat-tordera-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montgat-Tordera Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.2-mile (5.2 km) path that takes about 1 hour 33 minutes to complete, offering views from Cornice Viewpoint. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31585147" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turó de Sant Mateu – Cornice Viewpoint loop from Premià de Dalt&lt;/a&gt;, which is 2.5 miles (4.0 km) long and typically takes just over an hour.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on hikes around Premià De Dalt?
A: Due to its elevated position, many hiking routes in Premià De Dalt offer spectacular panoramic views. You can expect to see the Mediterranean Sea, the village itself, and the surrounding mountains. From higher points like Turó de Sant Mateu or Turó d'en Baldiri, you might even spot the Pyrenees, Montseny, the plain of Barcelona, and on clear days, the island of Mallorca.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Premià De Dalt?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31585147" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turó de Sant Mateu – Cornice Viewpoint loop from Premià de Dalt&lt;/a&gt;, which guides you through the hills of Serralada Litoral Natural Park. Another excellent loop is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33800552" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Turó d'en Baldiri – Turó de Sant Mateu loop from Premià de Dalt&lt;/a&gt;, offering diverse scenery.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or natural features to see while hiking?
A: While hiking in Premià De Dalt, you can explore various points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3174771" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Coscollada Observation Tower&lt;/a&gt; offers great views, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2830271" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iberian Site of Les Maleses&lt;/a&gt; provides a glimpse into ancient history. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2807997" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Can Boquet Cross&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3528179" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Creu de Montcabrer Summit&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the terrain like on the hiking trails in Premià De Dalt?
A: The terrain in Premià De Dalt is diverse, ranging from easily accessible paths to routes requiring good fitness and sure-footedness. You'll find extensive forests, primarily of pines and oaks, and varied elevation gains as you traverse the hills of the Serralada Litoral Natural Park.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Premià De Dalt?
A: The hiking routes in Premià De Dalt are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 150 reviews. Hikers often praise the mountainous coastal setting, the extensive forests, and the panoramic Mediterranean views. The variety of trails catering to different ability levels is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes in the area?
A: Yes, for those seeking a more challenging experience, there are moderate to difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1319872904/from-vallromanes-to-ermita-de-sant-mateu-del-bosc" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Vallromanes to Ermita de Sant Mateu del Bosc&lt;/a&gt; trail is a moderate 6.5-mile (10.5 km) path that winds through the natural park's forests towards a historic hermitage, offering a good workout with significant elevation changes.

Q: Is Premià De Dalt a good destination for hiking in winter?
A: Premià De Dalt can be a pleasant destination for winter hiking, as the Mediterranean climate generally offers milder temperatures compared to more inland or northern regions. The extensive forests of pines and oaks provide natural shelter, and clear winter days can offer crisp, far-reaching views of the coastline and mountains. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to a specific viewpoint?
A: Many trails in Premià De Dalt offer stunning viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33788953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turó de Sant Mateu – Font de Sant Mateu loop from Premià de Dalt&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent option, leading you to elevated spots with rewarding vistas. The Cornice Viewpoint, accessible via routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1323764376/montgat-tordera-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montgat-Tordera Trail&lt;/a&gt;, is also a popular spot for scenic overlooks.

Q: Can I find trails that connect Premià De Dalt to other nearby areas?
A: Yes, some trails connect Premià De Dalt to neighboring areas. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1319872904/from-vallromanes-to-ermita-de-sant-mateu-del-bosc" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Vallromanes to Ermita de Sant Mateu del Bosc&lt;/a&gt; route starts from Vallromanes and leads into the natural park, showcasing the interconnectedness of the region's trail network.
tips:
This space for rest and privileged viewpoint at the foot of Sant Mateu, is enabled with benches and parking support for bikes, and car parking. There is a panoramic sign that locates, with the corresponding place names, the hills and peaks that surround this point of view, both in the Maresme and Barcelonès, and in the Vallès Oriental and Occidental. From this point you can see the mountains of Montjuïc and Tibidabo, La Conreria, the Teià valley and the hills on the Vallesana slope, as well as being able to contemplate Montserrat, La Mola and Sant Llorenç del Munt.
   The viewpoint was inaugurated in 2004.

The Can Gurguí Fountain is located in the Parc de la Serralada Litoral, which always flows, little or a lot, with water from the Can Gurguí mine.

   Around the fountain there are stone benches and a round table. For the gloomy surroundings, the freshness of the water and the ease of access, it is one of the most visited sources in the Parc de la Serralada Litoral. In front of the fountain and on the other side of the track, there are a couple of poplars and a lush stream full of plane trees. Behind, there is a thick oak forest. Nearby is Can Gurguí, a former convent and now a restaurant.

  A stone front from 1899 supports the sandstone margin and supports the metal door of the mouth of the mine. The wide spout comes out from under the door and the water that flows is considered sanitary to drink.

It is a very simple Romanesque-style hermitage, with a single nave covered with a barrel vault, with a semicircular apse, which has a double-flow window. The nave is very short, which suggests that it was shortened during the restoration,

We must highlight the arrangement of the belfry bell tower, perpendicular to the façade, for its originality. Next to the chapel is the hermit's house.

In the 15th century it was known as San Mateo del Bosque and was property of the Priory of Sant Miquel del Fai.

The chapel has been documented since the year 993. In a pastoral visit from 1498 it is recorded that it is in a ruinous state but it had to be restored shortly after since in the 16th century it had worship again.

In the 16th century restoration, the nave had to be cut off, now quite short in relation to the apse, and with a very simple façade.

On Saint Matthew's Day, its traditional pilgrimage is celebrated.

From the hermitage you can enjoy good views of the Maresme, an area frequented by several hiking and mountain biking routes.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Montgat-Tordera Trail is rated as easy. It's a relatively short hike with moderate elevation changes, making it accessible for most fitness levels. The terrain around Montgat often features accessible paths, and the Tordera River section is generally easy to moderate.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. Starting near Montgat, you might encounter paths with coastal views or vineyard-dotted hillsides. As you progress towards the Tordera River, the landscape transitions to a more tranquil riverine environment. Expect a mix of accessible paths, potentially some unpaved sections, and diverse scenery.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several notable spots. Keep an eye out for the Cornice Viewpoint, offering scenic vistas. You can also visit the Can Gurguí Spring, a natural monument, and the historic Hermitage of Sant Mateu del Bosc.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Montgat-Tordera Trail intersects with several other paths. Significant portions of the route overlap with the Catalunya E16, GR 92 BTT, El Meridià Verd, and Ruta de l'Esquirol trails. You'll also find sections that coincide with La Font de Can Gurguí and Camí de la Cornisa.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for hiking in this region, offering mild temperatures and beautiful scenery. While possible in summer, be prepared for warmer weather, especially in open areas. Winter can also be enjoyable on clear days, but always check the forecast.

faqs.question: Is the Montgat-Tordera Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, as parts of it pass through Parc de la Serralada de Marina, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all users. Always carry water for your pet and clean up after them.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Montgat-Tordera Trail. It is freely accessible to the public. However, please respect the natural environment and any local regulations, especially within Parc de la Serralada de Marina.

faqs.question: Where can I park or access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail officially starts from Coll de Can Gurguí. While specific parking details for this exact starting point aren't provided, areas around Montgat and near the Tordera River often have public parking options. For public transport, check local bus or train routes connecting to Montgat or towns along the Tordera River, such as Tordera itself.

faqs.question: What are the regulations for hiking in Parc de la Serralada de Marina?
faqs.answer: As the trail passes through Parc de la Serralada de Marina, hikers should adhere to park regulations. This typically includes staying on marked paths, not disturbing wildlife or plants, carrying out all trash, and being mindful of fire restrictions, especially during dry seasons. Dogs should be kept on a leash.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: The Montgat-Tordera Trail is primarily a point-to-point route, connecting the Montgat area to the Tordera River region. While there are various local loops around Montgat, this specific named trail implies a linear journey. Plan for transportation at your end point or arrange to hike it as an out-and-back if you prefer.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or amenities nearby?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is a natural experience, you'll find amenities in the towns at either end or nearby. Montgat offers coastal amenities, and towns along the Tordera River will have cafes, pubs, and accommodation options. It's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks for the duration of your hike.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of Sant Mateu del Bosc
